content:
## Memory Archive and Project Organization

### Digital Memory Archive Structure ($ARCHIVE_ROOT)
A cornerstone of continuity in this OS is the **Digital Memory Archive**, rooted at the environment path **$ARCHIVE_ROOT**. This archive is the durable, long-term memory of the system – an ever-growing collection of files that record the evolutionary history of all creative endeavors within the OS. Unlike volatile chat histories or ad-hoc documents, the archive is organized and structured to be navigable by both humans and AI, even decades in the future, *without requiring any external database*. It achieves this via careful file organization, naming conventions, and index files.

Under $ARCHIVE_ROOT, we maintain several subdirectories that correspond to different dimensions of memory:

- **$ARCHIVE_ROOT/sessions/** – This folder contains logs of interactive sessions (conversations between human and AI, or multi-agent dialogues) that contributed to the OS’s development. Each session log is typically a markdown or text file, named with a timestamp or session ID for chronological sorting (e.g., `2025-11-23_session-00001.md` or a similar scheme including date-time). Within each session file, the content includes the dialogue (prompts and responses), and possibly embedded metadata like the final summary or outcome. Sessions are key for tracing how decisions were made or how knowledge was obtained. A manifest file at `$ARCHIVE_ROOT/sessions/index.txt` lists all sessions with basic info: session ID, date, participants (agents/humans), and a one-line summary or tags (e.g., “Brainstorming naming conventions”, “World Alpha – character design round”). This allows an AI agent to quickly search for past relevant sessions by keyword or ID without loading everything.
